item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was the 237 terminated? I dont think a Foundation agent would insist on leaving. She would know that she would be terminated. I don't think that part makes much sense.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced, and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

The tale series Parable of the Wayward Prince can be called the closest thing the Alexylva University GoI has to a hub page, and serves a great place for anyone interested in reading about AU skips and tales. Just as with SCP-1080, and SCP-1081 before it, linking this skip there would help facilitate such a leg of the wikiwalk, As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

ALEXYLVA UNIVERSITY PHILOSOPHY DEPT

To this:

ALEXYLVA UNIVERSITY PHILOSOPHY DEPT

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I find some tone issues and several things that can be greatly improved. A good idea and an upvote if fixed. -1 For now.
P.D: I'll gladly give it a try if allowed to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know why this scip received so much flak. It's really interesting, more interesting that some highly upvoted scips even. Sometimes I get surprised when I see some bland, silly scips get really good critics while other beautiful, well written ones are buried in downvotes or just generally ignored. I think it's sad, but maybe it's just me.

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Serpent's Hand involvement seems pretty unnecessary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. Upvoted, but, this. Is it just because she wasn't an "official" test subject that they wanted her to be dis-affected? Doesn't make a ton of sense to me, if she was already working for the Foundation, and as more than a D-class at that…besides, people who are highly logical and not very emotional make excellent Foundation personnel - think of Procedure whateverfucknumber(110?)-Montauk.

…Also, I've had a lot to say about the scips I've been reading lately…I'm really not trying to be a blabbermouth, honest. ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Executing the researcher seems a bit strange, even with not wanting them to be released to the outside world they'd probably come in useful so keeping them locked up seems more logical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw nothing.

F'real though, the timing made me doubt my sanity for a second.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't say that. It has never said that.

it maybe used to say that until here a minute ago

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Nitpicky little peeve that kept me from voting — quartz cannot be both clear and smokey, or for that matter, blue and smokey . Smokey quartz refers specifically to quartz that has a grey-brown color, that ranges from slightly cloudy to totally opaque.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Alexsylva University is my favorite group of interest, but unfortunately this article is a downvote for me. The tone seems to be way off.

item.title: Platon makes you too dumb for duty?
item.content:encoded: Furthermore, how would a single-digit decrease in iq even change someone from suitable to unsuitble for Foundation work? A 90 will generally be able to do anything a 99 could, not to mention such small gaps between scores well above average {like 133-142}. If the poor sod is so far below average that this 9 point loss just pushed them into official retardation… well, i doubt the Foundation would have hired them in the first place.

item.title: The word you are looking for is
item.content:encoded: Volition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the third bland SCP item I've seen with interesting University stuff attached. Once again, only the University stuff has saved this from a downvote from me.

Please, work on fixing these articles. The more bland things get attached to the University, the less interesting the University will be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's pretty much exactly right. I just couldn't think of how that would be measured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed. Perhaps we can indicate that they lose a large degree of creativity or capability to act without orders (I know there's a word that sums up the latter, but I'm having one of those scary days where basic vocabulary is fleeting) as a result of whatever is in the skull brutally reshaping their brain to think logically?

The basic premise I'm seeing here is that the University, highly valuing logic, commissioned this item with the idea that it could be used to create a more perfect man, a logical and rational man. Instead, they are finding that they are all too weak, and that a logical mind actually recoils from their principles. Since they cannot admit that their philosophy is wrong, instead they blame the item. Does that sound about right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good. The warrior thing sort of messes with my idea of Alexsylva, though I think that has more to do with the universe they're from than the university itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather like it, but there are points where the language used was more of a narrative then a scientific document. It needs some tightening up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I NEARLY downvoted at the picture because I thought it was going to be a crystal skull and I wanted to punish you for reminding me of the movie.

Then I read it and thought the article was Okay. Not Great, but Good Enough to deserve and upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't ring true for me at all:

Affected subjects typically register a decrease in IQ score of between █-██ points, rendering them unsuitable for continued employment with the Foundation. In spite of this, subjects will demonstrate greater reasoning skills and use of logic, though their actual knowledge and memories will be lessened.

That's not how IQ works, it has nothing to do with general knowledge or remembering facts. What you described would actually be an INCREASE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, the downvote kinda gave it away.

It's cool. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, it was more that I had the idea and didn't feel like I could do other stuff until I had it down.

Dude, that's like all of my SCPs ever. Writing is less of a hobby for me than it is a compulsion.

Oh, and this really doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, it was more that I had the idea and didn't feel like I could do other stuff until I had it down. But you're right, either way. This was the last University one that I really felt inspired to write, so it'll be a while before I get back to doing SCPs about them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mm. Don't care for this one much.

Think you need to slow down, take a breather. You're pumping out an alexylva university SCP every few days it feels. Your quantity is rising but quality dropping. I love the university items in general but the last few have felt more and more forced and less refined, like you were trying to meet some personal quota for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait what other one has a person inside it that does stuff? theres the sundial but i thought it was implied that was just a guy who wasn't recorded in history books, not a Alexylva staff member, either way i'm all for the expansion of the University mythos!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the upside, the picture made me laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neither the existence of crystal as an artistic medium nor the existence of skulls are original ideas to Indiana Jones. And I've never seen the movies, so…is this similar to the plot? I didn't use the phrase "crystal skull" because I thought it sounded vaguely cliche, but it really is just a side note.

I'm okay with someone not liking the SCP, but that's one of the strangest reasons for doing so I've ever heard of.

EDIT: Also, why would I reference a movie everybody hated apparently hated less than I thought when referencing any preexisting cultural creation is pretty much an automatic downvote?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're aware that Indiana Jones did not invent this concept, yes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I'm just seeing that this could somewhat be seen as a reference to Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really see a problem with it being made out of crystal. It's a tiny detail to the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe quartz is crystalline, yes. You prefer something more metallic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So….this is a "Crystal Skull"? Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not at all. This is at least the second University SCP that is "Thing with a person inside who does stuff." The idea that most of the staff or professors of Alexylva have no physical body is brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not much, it comes to me the idea of making the skull a non-sapient, automated item, created by someone or something, but that would require scrapping hte entire concept of the entity.

What makes the genius inside a bad idea is that it seems to be too powerful (at least for my liking)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't thought of 1083-1 as a "benevolent genius", per se. He/it doesn't know how to do anything except judge actions based on logical ability, and he/it's more than willing to drive people insane to suit his/its purposes. Do you have any specific suggestions for improvement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like it much, the idea of a benevolent genious doens't fit well, honestly.
Thought it can improved, so im downvoting until revision
